steps.title: Set up the per-acre inequality
steps.content: The arborist estimates at least 50 and no more than 60 hickory trees per acre: $50 \leq \text{trees per acre} \leq 60$.

steps.title: Scale to 3 acres
steps.content: Multiply every part of the inequality by 3: $50 \times 3 = 150$ and $60 \times 3 = 180$, giving $150 \leq x \leq 180$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $50 \leq x \leq 60$: These are the per-acre bounds, not scaled to 3 acres. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — $150 \leq x \leq 180$: Lower bound $50 \times 3 = 150$, upper bound $60 \times 3 = 180$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • Choice C — $50 \leq x \leq 180$: Lower bound is unscaled while upper bound is scaled; inconsistent. Eliminated.
  • Choice D — $150 \leq x \leq 60$: Lower bound is greater than upper bound, which is impossible. Eliminated.
